ref: Other seeds fell on thin, rocky ground and quickly started growing because the soil wasn't very deep.
text: Ti basɛba lu tahi zi'an, zi'an ti tintɔnɔ ka zo'e, Ti ba yehe kalam, tintɔnɔ en ka zo'e la zuo.
translated: If you want to get up early, get up early; if you want to sleep late, sleep late.

ref: And now your temple will be deserted.
text: Bihɛ, fum yire la n bala nyaa lebege daboo zɛ'a wa.
translated: Come, let us go and bring back the child that was left behind.

ref: and said, “This man claimed he could tear down God's temple and build it again in three days.”
text: yeti, “Nɛrekana wa yeti, eŋa wan ta'am gbi' Yinɛ pu'uhegɔ yire wa bahɛ gee lebe mɛ dɛ dabeha atã puan.”
translated: The yeti said, “My mother the yeti, I have a strong stomach. I can eat all the food that you people normally eat.”

ref: Go out to the street corners and tell everyone you meet to come to the banquet.”
text: Kiŋɛ ya kɛ̃ tiŋa la puan, ta doohe sadɔkãra la woo zuto la ya yi' yan nyɛ se'em woo tari wa'am pɔgedire malema la puan na.’
translated: The one who has a bad heart, when he gets power, he will oppress people and do bad things to them, and he will not have mercy on them.

ref: What the farmer is spreading is really the message about the kingdom.
text: Kaara la en lobege sɛla la dela yelehum la.
translated: The man and his wife were very happy.
